plan_units.learning_objective: Recognize that core physician traits directly map to innovation leadership traits, positioning clinicians as capable innovators within healthcare systems.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From seeing innovation as foreign to recognizing physicianship as innovation leadership through trait mapping.
plan_units.rationale: This is the conceptual opener for Part 3 and establishes the learner identity shift needed before introducing process frameworks or organizational context.
source_refs: core traits required for both physicianship and innovation
plan_units.title: Physicianship = Innovation Leadership

plan_units.learning_objective: Explain how the clinical method maps to design thinking so learners can see innovation as an extension of everyday diagnostic work.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From viewing design thinking as business jargon to understanding it as a familiar clinical reasoning process through direct workflow mapping.
plan_units.rationale: This is a high-value bridge concept with multiple explanatory beats and deserves progressive disclosure to prevent overload while preserving the process comparison.
source_refs: You run this innovation cycle twenty times a day
plan_units.title: The Bridge: Diagnosis = Design Thinking

plan_units.learning_objective: Identify the direct correspondences between history-taking, diagnosis, treatment planning, and the design thinking stages.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From viewing design thinking as business jargon to understanding it as a familiar clinical reasoning process through direct workflow mapping.
plan_units.rationale: This interstitial isolates the core analogies so learners can encode the mapping before broader application to systems problems.
source_refs: When you take a patient's history
the first step of Design Thinking: Empathize
you are performing the second step: Define
plan_units.title: Clinical Method to Design Thinking: Step Mapping

plan_units.learning_objective: Reframe innovation work as applying existing clinical reasoning to broken workflows and systems rather than only to individual patients.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From viewing design thinking as business jargon to understanding it as a familiar clinical reasoning process through direct workflow mapping.
plan_units.rationale: This interstitial completes the bridge by shifting the target of clinical reasoning from patient care to operational improvement.
source_refs: to a broken workflow or system
plan_units.title: From Patient Problems to System Problems

plan_units.learning_objective: Identify intrapreneurship as a structured, replicable approach to change within existing organizations rather than a vague personality trait.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From treating intrapreneurship as vague to understanding it as a structured internal change formula through guided reading and framing.
plan_units.rationale: The source explicitly requires this reading block before Slide 3 and frames later instruction on stakeholder alignment and organizational fit.
source_refs: a calculated approach to driving change from within
plan_units.title: Required Reading: Sandra Lam’s Intrapreneurship Formula

plan_units.learning_objective: Differentiate entrepreneurship from intrapreneurship and explain why clinicians often create the most immediate impact from within their health systems.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From conflating entrepreneurship with innovation to distinguishing intrapreneurship as the clinician's primary arena through contrastive definition.
plan_units.rationale: This comparison is a gestalt two-up distinction with high value in simultaneous contrast, so it should remain a keep-dense singleton.
source_refs: improve it from the inside out
plan_units.title: Intrapreneurship vs. Entrepreneurship

plan_units.learning_objective: Understand why intrapreneurship is harder than entrepreneurship because innovation must occur within legacy institutional constraints.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From assuming internal innovation is straightforward to appreciating the constraint-rich reality of hospital intrapreneurship through the load-bearing blueprint metaphor.
plan_units.rationale: The blueprint metaphor carries multiple constraints and a critical systems lesson, so progressive disclosure helps learners feel the stakes without flattening the metaphor.
source_refs: innovate without destroying the existing, load-bearing institutional structures
plan_units.title: The Intrapreneur’s Maze: Building Inside a Load-Bearing System

plan_units.learning_objective: Contrast the freedom of entrepreneurs with the embedded constraints faced by intrapreneurs in hospital settings.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From assuming internal innovation is straightforward to appreciating the constraint-rich reality of hospital intrapreneurship through the load-bearing blueprint metaphor.
plan_units.rationale: This interstitial isolates the central contrast so the metaphor does not get lost inside the fuller hospital-constraint image.
source_refs: You do not have that luxury
inside a highly complex, load-bearing blueprint
plan_units.title: Blank Canvas vs. Blueprint

plan_units.learning_objective: Explain that effective clinical innovation requires working with institutional realities such as IT, compliance, politics, and budgets.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From assuming internal innovation is straightforward to appreciating the constraint-rich reality of hospital intrapreneurship through the load-bearing blueprint metaphor.
plan_units.rationale: This resolve interstitial lands the practical implication of the metaphor and prepares learners for later stakeholder and execution work.
source_refs: redesign the room without breaking the building
plan_units.title: Redesign the Room Without Breaking the Building

plan_units.learning_objective: Distinguish a raw idea from a vetted opportunity by using the criteria of feasibility, viability, desirability, and path to execution.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From generating ideas casually to engineering actionable opportunities through disciplined vetting and organizational fit criteria.
plan_units.rationale: This is a central threshold concept for the module and should be decomposed because it contains definition, criteria, and execution implications.
source_refs: An idea is simply a thought or a suggestion
rigorously vetted for feasibility, viability, and desirability
It has a clear path to execution
plan_units.title: Idea vs. Opportunity

plan_units.learning_objective: Recognize that solution-first statements are not yet opportunities until they are grounded in organizational reality and execution logic.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From generating ideas casually to engineering actionable opportunities through disciplined vetting and organizational fit criteria.
plan_units.rationale: This interstitial clarifies the course’s strict definition and helps learners abandon superficial ideation habits.
source_refs: We should use AI to write notes
An idea is simply a thought
On its own, its value is zero
plan_units.title: Why Ideas Alone Have Zero Value

plan_units.learning_objective: Explain the characteristics that make an idea actionable as an intrapreneurial opportunity within a specific organization.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From generating ideas casually to engineering actionable opportunities through disciplined vetting and organizational fit criteria.
plan_units.rationale: This resolve interstitial lands the operational definition learners must use in later problem-framing work.
source_refs: goal is to stop generating ideas
plan_units.title: Opportunity = Feasible, Viable, Desirable, Executable

plan_units.learning_objective: Explain how first principles thinking helps healthcare innovators move beyond analogy-based patchwork improvements toward fundamentally redesigned systems.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From patching the status quo to rebuilding better systems through first-principles deconstruction and reconstruction.
plan_units.rationale: The three-phase progression has multiple explanatory beats and strong pedagogical weight, making it well-suited for clustering.
source_refs: Do not just iterate on 'how we have always done it'
Building a new system from the ground up
plan_units.title: First Principles Thinking: The Deconstruction

plan_units.learning_objective: Identify the limits of analogy-based thinking in healthcare improvement efforts.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From patching the status quo to rebuilding better systems through first-principles deconstruction and reconstruction.
plan_units.rationale: This interstitial surfaces the flawed default mindset before introducing the reconstructive value of first principles.
source_refs: try to just add a fresh coat of paint
plan_units.title: Reasoning by Analogy Keeps the Old System Intact

plan_units.learning_objective: Describe the sequence of deconstructing workflows into fundamental truths and reconstructing better systems from the same raw materials.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From patching the status quo to rebuilding better systems through first-principles deconstruction and reconstruction.
plan_units.rationale: This interstitial resolves the thinking model into a practical redesign sequence learners can reuse.
source_refs: build a fundamentally better, more efficient system
plan_units.title: Break to Fundamental Truths, Then Rebuild

plan_units.learning_objective: Apply rigorous problem framing by contrasting a superficial solution-first idea with a root-cause-based opportunity statement.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From superficial solutioning to disciplined opportunity framing through worked comparison and root-cause analysis.
plan_units.rationale: This is the module’s main application example and benefits from a full explanatory cluster because learners must compare, diagnose, and reframe.
plan_units.title: Worked Example: From Bad Idea to Good Opportunity

plan_units.learning_objective: Identify why jumping directly to a technology purchase is a weak starting point for innovation work.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From superficial solutioning to disciplined opportunity framing through worked comparison and root-cause analysis.
plan_units.rationale: This tension interstitial makes the common failure mode explicit and prepares learners for deeper diagnosis.
source_refs: buy iPads for all the nurses
plan_units.title: The Bad Start: Solution Before Problem

plan_units.learning_objective: Analyze the underlying workflow issue causing delayed discharges rather than treating the symptom with a premature solution.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From superficial solutioning to disciplined opportunity framing through worked comparison and root-cause analysis.
plan_units.rationale: This develop interstitial deepens the example by isolating the system diagnosis at the heart of the reframing.

Nurses do not have a standardized, easily accessible checklist
causing redundant trips to the nursing station

plan_units.learning_objective: Formulate an opportunity statement that ties root cause to a concrete workflow redesign aim.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From superficial solutioning to disciplined opportunity framing through worked comparison and root-cause analysis.
plan_units.rationale: This resolve interstitial provides the model output learners should emulate in their own work.
source_refs: design a standardized, mobile-friendly discharge workflow
plan_units.title: The Opportunity Statement

plan_units.learning_objective: Synthesize the module’s three main takeaways: clinicians are already equipped to innovate, intrapreneurship is the arena, and opportunities matter more than ideas.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From dispersed concepts to a coherent innovation stance through concise synthesis and transition.
plan_units.rationale: This is the formal recap and transition slide; its instructional value is the simultaneous whole, so it should remain a keep-dense singleton.
plan_units.title: Part 3 Summary and Transition

research_goals.pedagogical_intent: Learner needs the primary-source basis for Sandra Lam’s intrapreneurship framing so the workbook can quote or paraphrase Chapter 4 accurately.
Learner needs a trustworthy reproduction or verified summary of the required formula and venn diagram from page 48 to support the workbook companion.
Learner needs the exact source basis for the externally referenced image or concept used in the intrapreneurship versus entrepreneurship comparison so workbook explanation remains properly grounded.
Learner needs any available source fidelity details for the referenced HII PowerPoint slide 23 image so the workbook can accurately explain the intended idea-versus-opportunity visual.

depth_delta.deferred_depth: Expanded side-by-side explanation of each clinical step and its design-thinking analogue, including why the mapping matters for workflow innovation.
depth_delta.retained_on_slide: The high-level process comparison and direct step labels.

exercises.answer_key_source_ref: When you take a patient's history
exercises.prompt_intent: Have learners explain in their own words why history-taking maps to empathize and diagnosis maps to define.

exercises.answer_key_source_ref: to a broken workflow or system
exercises.prompt_intent: Ask learners to transfer the framework from patient care to a broken workflow they know.

sections.narrative_intent: Reinforce that innovation is not foreign work but a transfer of familiar clinical reasoning into operational contexts.
sections.title: Clinical Reasoning as Design Thinking

depth_delta.deferred_depth: Reading guide notes that help learners track how Lam turns intrapreneurship into a structured internal-change formula, with emphasis on resource leverage and stakeholder alignment.
depth_delta.retained_on_slide: Required reading cue and framing value.

exercises.prompt_intent: Prompt learners to summarize the difference between personality-based and formula-based views of intrapreneurship.

sections.narrative_intent: Support close reading so the external text becomes usable course scaffolding rather than a disconnected assignment.
sections.title: Reading Companion: The Intrapreneurship Formula

depth_delta.deferred_depth: A fuller unpacking of the institutional constraints named in the visual metaphor and how each can shape implementation risk.
depth_delta.retained_on_slide: The entrepreneur versus intrapreneur contrast and load-bearing blueprint metaphor.

exercises.prompt_intent: Have learners sort a proposed innovation challenge by likely constraint category: IT, compliance, politics, or budget.
Ask learners to explain why swinging a metaphorical sledgehammer can destabilize the larger system.

sections.narrative_intent: Deepen the systems-awareness posture needed for realistic internal innovation planning.
sections.title: The Constraint Map of Hospital Intrapreneurship

depth_delta.deferred_depth: A structured worksheet for testing ideas against feasibility, viability, desirability, organizational fit, and execution path.
depth_delta.retained_on_slide: The headline distinction between idea and opportunity.

exercises.answer_key_source_ref: rigorously vetted for feasibility, viability, and desirability
exercises.prompt_intent: Ask learners to convert a vague solution statement into an opportunity hypothesis using the course criteria.
Check recall of the course claim that ideas alone have no intrinsic value.

sections.narrative_intent: Make the core threshold concept operational so learners can use it in later assignments.
sections.title: From Idea to Opportunity Worksheet

depth_delta.deferred_depth: A fuller narrative explanation of analogy versus first-principles reasoning, using the block-building metaphor as a stepwise redesign method.
depth_delta.retained_on_slide: The three-stage visual progression from old system to raw blocks to new system.

exercises.prompt_intent: Have learners separate inherited workflow assumptions from fundamental truths in a familiar clinical process.

exercises.answer_key_source_ref: build a fundamentally better, more efficient system
exercises.prompt_intent: Ask learners to sketch a rebuilt process using the same core resources arranged differently.

sections.narrative_intent: Shift learners from patching inherited workflows to reconstructing systems from first principles.
sections.title: First Principles Deconstruction Notes

depth_delta.deferred_depth: A detailed annotation of the bad idea, root-cause diagnosis, and final opportunity statement, showing each move in the reframing process.
depth_delta.retained_on_slide: The Good vs. Bad contrast and final opportunity statement.

exercises.prompt_intent: Ask learners to identify where a solution-first statement fails to define the real problem.

exercises.answer_key_source_ref: design a standardized, mobile-friendly discharge workflow
exercises.prompt_intent: Have learners rewrite a superficial complaint into a root-cause-based opportunity statement.

sections.narrative_intent: Provide the slow, inspectable reasoning path that the slide deck only gestures at, enabling transfer to learner-generated cases.
sections.title: Worked Example Annotated Walkthrough

depth_delta.deferred_depth: Brief rationales and concept reminders behind each knowledge-check item so learners can correct misunderstandings before moving on.

sections.narrative_intent: Turn the end-of-part quiz into a retrieval-plus-feedback tool that consolidates the module's vocabulary and distinctions.
sections.title: Knowledge Check Review Guide

lesson_plan.lesson_summary: Part 3 reframes clinicians as already equipped innovators, maps diagnostic practice to design thinking, distinguishes intrapreneurship from entrepreneurship, and pushes learners from raw ideas toward rigorously framed opportunities through first-principles thinking and a worked example. Clustering is used where concept density and pedagogical weight are highest; synthesis, comparisons, and recap slides are kept dense as singletons.

plan_units.learning_objective: Recognize that core physician traits directly map to innovation leadership traits, positioning clinicians as capable innovators within healthcare systems.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From seeing innovation as foreign to recognizing physicianship as innovation leadership through trait mapping.
plan_units.rationale: This is the conceptual opener for Part 3 and establishes the learner identity shift needed before introducing process frameworks or organizational context.
source_refs: core traits required for both physicianship and innovation
plan_units.title: Physicianship = Innovation Leadership

plan_units.learning_objective: Explain how the clinical method maps to design thinking so learners can see innovation as an extension of everyday diagnostic work.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From viewing design thinking as business jargon to understanding it as a familiar clinical reasoning process through direct workflow mapping.
plan_units.rationale: This is a high-value bridge concept with multiple explanatory beats and deserves progressive disclosure to prevent overload while preserving the process comparison.
source_refs: You run this innovation cycle twenty times a day
plan_units.title: The Bridge: Diagnosis = Design Thinking

plan_units.learning_objective: Identify the direct correspondences between history-taking, diagnosis, treatment planning, and the design thinking stages.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From viewing design thinking as business jargon to understanding it as a familiar clinical reasoning process through direct workflow mapping.
plan_units.rationale: This interstitial isolates the core analogies so learners can encode the mapping before broader application to systems problems.
source_refs: When you take a patient's history
the first step of Design Thinking: Empathize
you are performing the second step: Define
plan_units.title: Clinical Method to Design Thinking: Step Mapping

plan_units.learning_objective: Reframe innovation work as applying existing clinical reasoning to broken workflows and systems rather than only to individual patients.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From viewing design thinking as business jargon to understanding it as a familiar clinical reasoning process through direct workflow mapping.
plan_units.rationale: This interstitial completes the bridge by shifting the target of clinical reasoning from patient care to operational improvement.
source_refs: to a broken workflow or system
plan_units.title: From Patient Problems to System Problems

plan_units.learning_objective: Identify intrapreneurship as a structured, replicable approach to change within existing organizations rather than a vague personality trait.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From treating intrapreneurship as vague to understanding it as a structured internal change formula through guided reading and framing.
plan_units.rationale: The source explicitly requires this reading block before Slide 3 and frames later instruction on stakeholder alignment and organizational fit.
source_refs: a calculated approach to driving change from within
plan_units.title: Required Reading: Sandra Lam’s Intrapreneurship Formula

plan_units.learning_objective: Differentiate entrepreneurship from intrapreneurship and explain why clinicians often create the most immediate impact from within their health systems.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From conflating entrepreneurship with innovation to distinguishing intrapreneurship as the clinician's primary arena through contrastive definition.
plan_units.rationale: This comparison is a gestalt two-up distinction with high value in simultaneous contrast, so it should remain a keep-dense singleton.
source_refs: improve it from the inside out
plan_units.title: Intrapreneurship vs. Entrepreneurship

plan_units.learning_objective: Understand why intrapreneurship is harder than entrepreneurship because innovation must occur within legacy institutional constraints.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From assuming internal innovation is straightforward to appreciating the constraint-rich reality of hospital intrapreneurship through the load-bearing blueprint metaphor.
plan_units.rationale: The blueprint metaphor carries multiple constraints and a critical systems lesson, so progressive disclosure helps learners feel the stakes without flattening the metaphor.
source_refs: innovate without destroying the existing, load-bearing institutional structures
plan_units.title: The Intrapreneur’s Maze: Building Inside a Load-Bearing System

plan_units.learning_objective: Contrast the freedom of entrepreneurs with the embedded constraints faced by intrapreneurs in hospital settings.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From assuming internal innovation is straightforward to appreciating the constraint-rich reality of hospital intrapreneurship through the load-bearing blueprint metaphor.
plan_units.rationale: This interstitial isolates the central contrast so the metaphor does not get lost inside the fuller hospital-constraint image.
source_refs: You do not have that luxury
inside a highly complex, load-bearing blueprint
plan_units.title: Blank Canvas vs. Blueprint
